context.projectOverview: Sprint 4 of 5. Deterministic cadence math (computeNextDue, clock-free) + idempotent `bober research tick` that runs only due jobs (nextDueAt <= injected now, or unset), advances nextDueAt and records lastRunAt, + optional --watch loop. Document scheduling tradeoff. Invokes Sprint 2 runResearchJob unchanged.

completedSprints.title: runner
completedSprints.summary: 20d42cb — tick invokes runResearchJob per due job

completedSprints.title: egress axis
completedSprints.summary: 0150737 — runner deps gained OPTIONAL egress/retrievalClient; tick passes deps through unchanged

principles: Read .bober/principles.md. Hard gates: strict TS zero errors; ZERO lint errors; ESM .js imports; Zod; async fs; CLOCK INJECTION — no Date.now()/new Date() inside cadence.ts or scheduler.ts cores; only at the CLI .action boundary (~research.ts:209).

curatorFindings.schemaGap: CONFIRMED: ResearchJobSchema (src/research/types.ts:33-54) does NOT have nextDueAt/lastRunAt. You MUST extend it with `nextDueAt: z.string().datetime().optional()` and `lastRunAt: z.string().datetime().optional()`. REASON: addJob does ResearchJobSchema.safeParse (job-store.ts:48) before write and z.object STRIPS unknown keys — without these fields, scheduler updates would be silently dropped. (This adds types.ts to the file set; contract estimatedFiles omitted it — that's expected.) Ensure computeNextDue returns a .datetime()-valid ISO string (Date.toISOString() satisfies it).
curatorFindings.persistPath: Scheduler persists an advanced job by reusing addJob as an in-place UPSERT (job-store.ts:42-60). jobId hashes ONLY question|createdAt (job-store.ts:28-33), so adding nextDueAt/lastRunAt does NOT change the id => re-adding the same id overwrites the same file. NO dedicated updateJob needed (but you may add a thin updateJob wrapper for clarity if it still routes through the stable id). Verify the id does NOT incorporate nextDueAt/lastRunAt.
curatorFindings.cadence: src/research/cadence.ts computeNextDue(cadence, fromIso): string — PURE, clock-free. Parse fromIso (Date.parse / new Date(fromIso) on the INJECTED instant is fine — it's not the wall clock), add daily=+1 day, weekly=+7 days, monthly=+1 month, return toISOString(). Mirror the pure Date math in src/calendar/slotter.ts. Watch month-length edges (use setUTCMonth or document the JS Date rollover behavior).
curatorFindings.schedulerAndCli: src/research/scheduler.ts tick(deps) where deps={now, runJob, jobStore (or load/list+persist fns)}: list all jobs, select nextDueAt<=now OR nextDueAt unset, invoke runJob(job) (=runResearchJob) per due job, then set job.lastRunAt=now, job.nextDueAt=computeNextDue(job.cadence, now), persist via addJob upsert. CLI: add `tick [--watch]` to research.ts injecting now=new Date().toISOString() at the .action boundary; --watch uses setInterval but the loop body must delegate to the same injected-now tick for testability. In tick help text DOCUMENT the tradeoff: in-repo --watch loop (simple, dies with process) vs OS cron/launchd calling `bober research tick` (survives reboots, RECOMMENDED for unattended) vs harness scheduler; note hosted-OAuth schedulers unfit for unattended runs.

notes: Contract: .bober/contracts/sprint-spec-20260628-research-scheduler-4.json. Briefing: .bober/briefings/sprint-spec-20260628-research-scheduler-4-briefing.md. NON-GOALS: no wall-clock read in cores; do NOT change how a single run executes (runResearchJob unchanged); no new egress (S3 owns it); no digest (S5); no distributed/locked scheduling; single-process. Idempotency is the key property: a 2nd tick at the SAME now must run ZERO jobs.
